sample_information.notes_instructions:
The HOA is requesting bacteriological and basic chemical analysis for three separate water sources within the Pacific Vista Condominiums complex:

1) Building A – Lobby Drinking Fountain (treated municipal water):
   - Sample ID: PV-BLDG-A-DF-2025-04-17
   - Collected from the main filtered drinking fountain in the ground-floor lobby of Building A, which is supplied by Guam Waterworks Authority (GWA) municipal treated water and passes through an in-line carbon filter maintained by Island Property Management.
   - Please test for total coliform, E. coli, and heterotrophic plate count, as well as basic physical/chemical parameters (pH, conductivity, turbidity, and free chlorine residual). We have had a few verbal complaints from residents about a slight chlorine taste and occasional musty odor in the evenings, particularly after heavy rain.

2) Building B – Unit 305 Kitchen Tap (treated municipal water):
   - Sample ID: PV-BLDG-B-305-KT-2025-04-17
   - Collected from the cold-water kitchen tap in Unit 305, Building B. The unit is on the third floor and is located on the end of the distribution line for that building, which may be a low-circulation zone.
   - Please test for total coliform and E. coli. The resident (family of four with two young children) reported intermittent cloudiness in the tap water when first turned on in the morning, which clears after about 20–30 seconds. They also mentioned a slight metallic taste over the past month.

3) Common Area Swimming Pool – Main Skimmer Line (recreational water):
   - Sample ID: PV-POOL-MAIN-2025-04-17
   - Sample collected from the main skimmer line in the center of the pool, approximately 30 cm below the surface, away from return jets. Pool is chlorinated using automatic dosing system with liquid chlorine.
   - Please test for total coliform and E. coli for recreational water quality screening. If possible under the standard panel, also note free and total chlorine, pH, and turbidity. The pool has seen increased usage over the last two months (especially by children) and we want to verify that our current disinfection regime is adequate.

General notes and handling:
- All samples were collected on 2025-04-17 in pre-sterilized containers provided by WERI. Chain-of-custody was maintained by the HOA representative (Daniel K. Santos) from time of collection until delivery to the WERI laboratory front desk on the same day.
- Samples were immediately placed on ice in a dedicated cooler and kept between approximately 4–8°C during transport. No chemical preservatives were added beyond what is already provided in the WERI sample bottles.
- Please notify the point of contact by email if any samples are deemed invalid, compromised, or received outside of recommended holding times so we can resample promptly.
- The HOA is particularly concerned about bacteriological safety for children and elderly residents who primarily use the lobby drinking fountain and the swimming pool. If results approach or exceed recommended thresholds, we would appreciate a brief written note with any recommended corrective actions or follow-up testing intervals.
- Preferred format for results: PDF report via email, with individual sample IDs clearly indicated and a short interpretation of whether each result meets applicable drinking water or recreational water guidelines (if available).
